title: leucanthemopsis alpina subsp. pyrenaica
intro: Leucanthemopsis alpina subsp. pyrenaica, also known as Pyrenean Cushion White, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to the Pyrenees mountain range. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and respiratory problems, due to its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Leucanthemopsis alpina subsp. pyrenaica include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support respiratory health. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
